page: Amazon Web Services r7gd.4xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance powered by the custom 64-bit ARM architecture. It features 16 dedicated physical cores utilizing the AWS Graviton Neoverse-V1 processor, running at a clock speed of 2.6 GHz. The CPU configuration includes 64 KB of L1D cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache, and 32 MB of L3 cache per core, with single-threaded execution per core. The instance is equipped with 128.0 GB of system memory, yielding an 8.0 GB memory-to-core ratio, and includes 950 GB of local NVMe SSD instance storage. Network performance is anchored by an 8 Gbps baseline bandwidth managed via the AWS Nitro hypervisor.

Benchmark data highlights strong memory performance, with the Passmark memory score ranking in the top tier (top 10%). Large-block uncached memory write bandwidth is also strong, reaching 97.85 GB/sec, while cached small-block read bandwidth registers at 1552.22 GB/sec. CPU performance is generally average, with a Geekbench multi-core score of 13044.0 and a stress-ng multi-core score of 24505.16. However, cryptographic performance is a relative bottleneck, with the Passmark CPU encryption test scoring in the bottom 25% at 2224.38. Similarly, LLM prompt processing with a medium 7B model is weak at 28.2 tokens/sec, though text generation remains average at 16.04 tokens/sec.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the r7gd.4xlarge offers a high-density memory profile combined with fast local NVMe storage, making it a viable alternative to x86-based memory-optimized instances for workloads optimized for ARM64. The inclusion of local NVMe SSDs eliminates the need for external block storage for high-throughput scratch space. This hardware configuration is well-suited for memory-intensive applications, database hosting, caching layers such as Redis (which achieves 25.97 million operations per second in testing), static web serving, and software compilation tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services r7gd.4xlarge is an ARM64 memory-optimized instance powered by 16 dedicated AWS Graviton Neoverse-V1 physical cores running at 2.6 GHz. It features 128.0 GB of RAM, providing a high 8.0 GB per core ratio, alongside 950 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and an 8 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, the instance delivers top-tier memory performance, with uncached memory write bandwidth reaching 97.85 GB/sec and a Passmark memory score in the top 10%. While CPU-bound cryptographic operations and medium-scale LLM prompt processing show lower relative performance, the instance excels in memory-intensive tasks. The combination of high memory capacity and fast local storage makes this server suitable for database operations, Redis caching, and memory-heavy general-purpose workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r7gd.4xlarge is an ARM64 memory-optimized instance featuring 16 dedicated Graviton cores, 128 GB of RAM, 950 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, and top-tier memory bandwidth performance.

tagline: An ARM-powered, memory-optimized instance featuring high-speed local NVMe storage and top-tier memory bandwidth for data-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: 16 dedicated ARM64 AWS Graviton Neoverse-V1 physical cores running at 2.6 GHz
128.0 GB of system memory delivering top-tier memory bandwidth performance
950 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD instance storage
8 Gbps baseline network bandwidth managed by the AWS Nitro hypervisor
Optimized for database hosting, Redis caching, and memory-intensive applications
